title: 山核桃大街谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\121-山核桃大街谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During an interrogation with Detective Sharp, Nigel Chapman describes his third method for obtaining poison to prove a point. Nigel explains that he disguised himself as an intern doctor by wearing a white coat and a stethoscope, which he had stolen from Len Bateson and subsequently pawned. He entered Celia’s pharmacy while a new employee was working the window, successfully posing as medical staff to obtain pills. Nigel claims he used these three methods—stealing from a car, forging a prescription under the name H.R. James, and the pharmacy disguise—to acquire scopolamine hydrobromide, hyoscine hydrochloride, and digitalis tincture.

Nigel asserts that he kept the scopolamine and hyoscine in a closet drawer under his socks and eventually disposed of all the poisons by burning them and flushing them down the toilet, a plan he discussed with Pat Lean. Detective Sharp suggests the possibility of spying, noting that someone could have entered Nigel's room, replaced the poison in the bottles with something else, and returned them. Nigel admits he lives with Len Bateson and that while girls are generally not allowed in the boys' dorm, Pat Lean visits his room to mend his socks. Nigel maintains his innocence regarding the murder of Silvia, acknowledging that his word alone is insufficient. Following the interrogation, Len Bateson and Colin McNab confirm the details of Nigel's bet and the disposal of the poison. Detective Sharp then speaks privately with Colin McNab regarding the poisoning of Colin's fiancée.
